title: DAB Detection of Biocytin Labeled Tissue
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3hfl2","text":"This protocol describes the process for diaminobenzidine (DAB) detection of biocytin filled cells.  This protocol is optimized for use with brain slices cut at 350 µm thick, in which cells are first filled with biocytin (i.e., post-electrophysiological recording), fixed in 4% PFA/2.5% glutaraldehyde, and transferred to PBS until ready to stain.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6bon7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5cff2","text":"Note: Research reported in this publication was supported by the National Institute Of Mental Health of the National Institutes of Health under Award Number U19MH114830. The content is solely the responsibility of the authors and does not necessarily represent the official views of the National Institutes of Health.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
